topics.name: 100–200 Level: Circuit Theory, Electronics, and Engineering Mathematics
topics.description: EEE 101 Circuit Theory I, EEE 201 Electronics I, MTH 211 Engineering Mathematics.

subtopics.name: EEE 101: DC and AC Circuit Analysis
objectives: Apply Kirchhoff's voltage and current laws to solve DC circuit problems
Analyse AC circuits using phasor notation, impedance, and power factor concepts

subtopics.name: EEE 201: Semiconductor Devices and Basic Electronics
objectives: Explain the operation of diodes, bipolar junction transistors (BJT), and field-effect transistors (FET)
Design basic rectifier, amplifier, and switching circuits using semiconductor devices

topics.name: 300 Level: Power Systems, Control Engineering, and Electromagnetic Fields
topics.description: EEE 301 Power Systems I, EEE 311 Control Systems, EEE 321 Electromagnetic Fields.

subtopics.name: EEE 301: Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution
objectives: Describe the components of Nigerian national grid and transmission line parameters
Calculate power losses, efficiency, and voltage regulation in transmission lines

subtopics.name: EEE 311: Classical Control Theory
objectives: Model dynamic systems using transfer functions and block diagrams
Analyse stability using Routh-Hurwitz criterion, Bode plots, and root locus methods

topics.name: 400–500 Level: Power Electronics, Machines, Digital Systems & Final Project
topics.description: EEE 401 Power Electronics, EEE 411 Electrical Machines, EEE 499 Final Year Project.

subtopics.name: EEE 401: Power Electronics and Drive Systems
objectives: Analyse and design thyristor, MOSFET, and IGBT-based converters and inverters
Design speed control systems for DC and AC motor drives using power electronic converters

subtopics.name: EEE 499: Final Year Engineering Project
objectives: Design, build, and test an electrical or electronic system solving a real engineering problem
Present engineering design report and defend project before FUNAI Engineering faculty board
